title: Amarnath Temple
tagline: Amarnath Temple — spiritual town of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Amarnath Temple is a Hindu shrine located in the Pahalgam tehsil of Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, India.
overview.description: Amarnath Temple is a Hindu shrine located in the Pahalgam tehsil of Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is a cave situated at an altitude of 3,888 m (12,756 ft), about 72 km from Anantnag, the district headquarters, 141 km (88 mi) from Srinagar, the summer capital of Jammu and Kashmir, accessible via either Sonamarg or Pahalgam The cave, located in the Sind Valley, is surrounded by glaciers and snow-covered mountains and remains inaccesssible for most of the year, opening to pilgrims only for a brief period in summer. In 1989, pilgrims numbered between 12,000 and 30,000. In 2011,

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Mount Amarnath
topPlaces.description: Mount Amarnath is a mountain with a peak elevation of 5,186 metres (17,014 ft), in the Ganderbal district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, in the vicinity of Sonamarg. Amarnath Peak is part of the Himalayas, and is located south of Zojila and west of Machoi Glacier.

topPlaces.name: Panchtarni
topPlaces.description: Panchtarni is a locality in Anantnag district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is a popular pilgrimage and tourist destination on the way to Amarnath Temple near the Amarnath Glacier in Himalayas.

topPlaces.name: Machoi Glacier
topPlaces.description: The Machoi Glacier is a 9 kilometer long glacier in the Himalayan Range in Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh, India.

topPlaces.name: Machoi Peak
topPlaces.description: Mechoi Peak is a mountain with a peak elevation of 17,907 ft (5,458 metres), in Drass region of Ladakh, India. Mechoi Peak is part of the Himalaya Range, and is located between Amarnath cave and Zojila.

topPlaces.name: Sind River
topPlaces.description: The Sind River or the Sindh River is a river in the Ganderbal district of the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is a major tributary of the Jhelum River and is 108 km (67 miles) long.

topPlaces.name: Zoji La
topPlaces.description: Zoji La (sometimes Zojila Pass) is a high mountain pass in the Himalayas. It is located in the Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir and the Kargil district of Ladakh, both union territories of India.

topPlaces.name: Zoji-la Tunnel
topPlaces.description: Zoji-La Tunnel, the world's longest single-tube bi-directional road tunnel at the highest altitude, is an under-construction Himalayan tunnel in India under the Zoji La pass between Sonamarg in Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir and Dras in Kargil district of Ladakh. This project is being executed by a Hyderabad-based engineering company, MEIL. It is 9.5 m wide, 7.57 m high and 13.153 km long, horseshoe-shaped single-tube, 2 lane road tunnel at the height of around 11,578 feet above sea level.

topPlaces.name: Meenamarg
topPlaces.description: Meenmarg is one the entry points in Ladakh from Jammu and Kashmir in India. It is just east of Zoji La Pass.

items.note: Mount Amarnath is a mountain with a peak elevation of 5,186 metres (17,014 ft), in the Ganderbal district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, in the vicinity of Sonamarg. Amarnath Peak is part of the Himalayas, and is located south of Zojila and west of Machoi Glacier.

Panchtarni is a locality in Anantnag district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is a popular pilgrimage and tourist destination on the way to Amarnath Temple near the Amarnath Glacier in Himalayas.
The Machoi Glacier is a 9 kilometer long glacier in the Himalayan Range in Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh, India.

Mechoi Peak is a mountain with a peak elevation of 17,907 ft (5,458 metres), in Drass region of Ladakh, India. Mechoi Peak is part of the Himalaya Range, and is located between Amarnath cave and Zojila.
The Sind River or the Sindh River is a river in the Ganderbal district of the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is a major tributary of the Jhelum River and is 108 km (67 miles) long.

Zoji La (sometimes Zojila Pass) is a high mountain pass in the Himalayas. It is located in the Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir and the Kargil district of Ladakh, both union territories of India.

Zoji-La Tunnel, the world's longest single-tube bi-directional road tunnel at the highest altitude, is an under-construction Himalayan tunnel in India under the Zoji La pass between Sonamarg in Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir and Dras in Kargil district of Ladakh. This project is being executed by a Hyderabad-based engineering company, MEIL. It is 9.5 m wide, 7.57 m high and 13.153 km long, horseshoe-shaped single-tube, 2 lane road tunnel at the height of around 11,578 feet above sea level.
Meenmarg is one the entry points in Ladakh from Jammu and Kashmir in India. It is just east of Zoji La Pass.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Amarnath Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Amarnath Temple is Apr – Jun, Sep – Oct. Summers are 10–22°C and winters are -6–10°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Amarnath Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (73 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (146 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Amarnath Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Amarnath Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Amarnath Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Amarnath Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Amarnath Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mount Amarnath, Panchtarni, Machoi Glacier, Machoi Peak, Sind River.
